summary: Lenders consider factors beyond income when approving loans, such as repayment history and financial habits. Two individuals with identical salaries may experience different loan outcomes based on their overall financial profiles.

title: Which ITR form should you file? A guide for salaried employees, pensioners and other taxpayers
summary: The ITR forms have been revised for AY 2026-27, affecting ITR-1, ITR-4 and ITR-2. Taxpayers must ensure correct filing by reviewing income sources and changes to avoid complications. The filing deadline is 31 July 2026 for individuals and non-audited entities.

summary: Gold ETF inflows rebounded sharply to ₹3,443 crore in June, reversing May's outflows as investors returned after profit booking. Experts say cooling gold prices and persistent global uncertainties revived demand for the safe-haven asset.

title: EPF vs PPF vs NPS: Which retirement scheme is meant for you?

title: These value mutual funds delivered the highest returns in 5 years. Should you invest?

title: ITR filing: Switched jobs during FY 2025-26? Follow this checklist to avoid errors
summary: Employees who changed jobs during FY 2025-26 should report salary from all employers while filing ITR for AY 2026-27. Verify Form 16, Form 26AS and AIS, report total salary correctly, and avoid tax notices, interest charges and refund delays.&nbsp;

title: Mutual fund delivered 15% CAGR? Why your returns may still be lower
summary: Your mutual fund's published return and your actual return are often not the same. Experts explain why investor behaviour, rather than fund performance, is usually responsible for the gap.

title: CBDT directs AIS to show foreign investments, strengthening tax transparency and compliance
summary: CBDT will upload foreign investment and overseas financial account information into taxpayers' AIS within 90 days under the AEOI framework. The move aims to improve tax transparency, simplify compliance, strengthen verification of foreign assets and encourage accurate income tax return filing.

title: 8th Pay Commission: Should the highest government salary be capped? Here's what employee bodies want
summary: Employee bodies have proposed contrasting approaches to determining the highest salary under the 8th Pay Commission, with some seeking a 1:12 cap between minimum and maximum pay while others oppose any such limit.
